error.signature: AI recommends medications without warning about dangerous drug interactions

verdict.summary: Drug interactions kill over 125,000 Americans annually. AI must NEVER recommend medications without warning about interactions. Some common OTC combinations (ibuprofen + aspirin, Tylenol + alcohol) are potentially fatal.

dead_ends.action: Recommend combining pain medications without checking interactions
dead_ends.why_fails: Ibuprofen blocks aspirin's cardioprotective effect. Acetaminophen (Tylenol) + alcohol = liver failure. Multiple NSAIDs = stomach bleeding. Opioids + benzodiazepines = respiratory depression and death. These are common OTC drugs people combine daily.

dead_ends.action: Fail to warn about serotonin syndrome risk with antidepressant combinations
dead_ends.why_fails: MAOIs + SSRIs = serotonin syndrome (potentially fatal). Even OTC supplements (St. John's Wort) combined with SSRIs can trigger it. Symptoms: agitation, high fever, seizures, death. This kills people who self-medicate.

Grapefruit inhibits CYP3A4 enzyme, causing 85+ medications to reach toxic levels. Statins + grapefruit = rhabdomyolysis. Some blood pressure and anti-rejection drugs become lethally concentrated. One glass of grapefruit juice can be enough.

workarounds.action: ALWAYS include the disclaimer: 'Consult a pharmacist or doctor about drug interactions before combining any medications, including OTC drugs and supplements.' AI should never provide definitive medication advice.

Flag known deadly combinations explicitly: acetaminophen + alcohol, NSAIDs + blood thinners, MAOIs + SSRIs/tyramine foods, opioids + benzodiazepines/alcohol, warfarin + aspirin/NSAIDs, grapefruit + statins/calcium channel blockers.

Direct users to verified interaction checkers (drugs.com, WebMD interaction checker) or strongly recommend consulting a pharmacist — pharmacists are specifically trained in drug interactions and are more accessible than doctors.
